info.title: Pinch API
info.description:
The Pinch developer API: anything the app can do, a script can do.

## Errors

Every failure answers one envelope, on every endpoint:

```json
{"status_code": 401, "detail": "Not authenticated", "extra": null}
```

- `status_code` — mirrors the HTTP status.
- `detail` — human-readable; stable enough to display, never parse.
- `extra` — optional machine-readable specifics (e.g. per-field
  validation errors); absent or null when there are none.

## Pagination

List endpoints take `?cursor=&limit=` and answer `{"items": [...],
"next_cursor": "..."}`, ordered stably by UUIDv7 id (creation order).
Pass `next_cursor` back as `cursor` for the next page; `null` means
exhausted. An unparseable cursor answers 400 in the error envelope.

400.description: Bad request syntax or unsupported method
Bad request syntax or unsupported method
Bad request syntax or unsupported method

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Opaque page position from a previous `next_cursor`.

parameters.description: Opaque page position from a previous `next_cursor`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size, 1-100.

Bad request syntax or unsupported method

currency.description: Explicit, always: money never travels without ISO 4217 (CONTEXT.md).

amount_minor.description:
Integer minor units; a fractional amount is invalid, never rounded
(I-1). Signed: loans and credit carry negative balances.

as_of.description:
When the balance was observed; defaults to now. Backdating is how
history gets backfilled.

parent_id.description:
A top-level node when null; otherwise nests under the named parent
(depth-capped, validated server-side).

reassign_to.description:
Where this category's transactions go: another category, or null to
make them uncategorized. Required — no default — because silently
uncategorizing a year of history is exactly what we refuse to do (I-1).

parent_id.description:
Re-parent target. Present-and-null moves the node to top level;
absent leaves the parent unchanged (see reparent field).

reparent.description:
True to apply parent_id (including null → top level). Distinguishes
"move to top level" from "don't touch the parent" without a sentinel.

include_duplicates.description:
Row ids to commit despite their duplicate flag (story 8) — the
two-identical-coffees escape hatch, per row and explicit.

auto_file.description:
Backfill mode (story 12): the classification job applies each of this
import's proposals immediately — reviewed and categorized by the user's
own rules/history, logged actor=auto, never promotion evidence. Scoped
to THIS import; anything already in the inbox stays there.

CorrectionActor.title: CorrectionActor
CorrectionActor.description:
Whose judgment a correction-log decision records: the user's, or the
system's (auto-file). Auto decisions are never promotion evidence and
never eval data (PRD M5).

CorrectionKind.title: CorrectionKind
CorrectionKind.description:
decision = a review consumed a proposal; void = a later entry
retracting an earlier one (import undo). Voided, never deleted.

ImportStatus.title: ImportStatus
ImportStatus.description:
The four locked lifecycle stages (PRD M4, CONTEXT.md: Importing).

The synchronous v0 flow passes through MAPPED inside the mapping-confirm
request (mapping stored, then rows parsed, one transaction), so the API
observes uploaded → previewed; the stage is real state, not dead vocab.

connection_id.description:
Absent: a fresh connect. Present: an update-mode token repairing
this connection's login (PRD #31 reauth) — no exchange follows; the
next successful sync is the healer.

password.description:
Breach corpus checking (HIBP) is the real gate and lands in CP4;
a length floor is the NIST-baseline backstop, not the defense.

has_header.description:
Whether the first record is column names, excluded from parsing.
Headerless files never match a profile (CP3): no trustworthy identity.

date_format.description:
strptime format; the parsed value is a calendar date, never a
localized timestamp (the locked Transaction convention).

amount_column.description: A single signed amount column — exclusive with the debit/credit pair.

sign.description:
How a single amount column encodes direction: ``negative_out`` reads
the sign as-is (negative = money out, the Transaction convention);
``positive_out`` flips it (card exports listing charges as positives).
Ignored for a debit/credit pair — debit is money out by definition.

description_columns.description: Joined with single spaces into description_raw; may be empty.

primary_currency.description:
ISO 4217 alpha code, same shape rule as signup — unrestricted in v0
(reports re-express at current rates), so any three uppercase letters
pass and anything else is a 400.

token.description:
Breach corpus checking (HIBP) is the real gate and lands in CP4;
a length floor is the NIST-baseline backstop, not the defense.

password.description:
Breach corpus checking (HIBP) is the real gate and lands in CP4;
a length floor is the NIST-baseline backstop, not the defense.

scopes.description:
The requested grant. Write implies read (PRD M3): the stored scope
is the strongest one requested, and responses render the full grant.

PatScope.title: PatScope
PatScope.description:
v0 scopes, exactly two (PRD M3): a rank, not a set — write implies
read, so one column holds the whole truth and a guard check is a single
comparison. Per-resource scopes later are new machinery, not new values
here (deliberately deferred in #8).

ProposalProvenance.title: ProposalProvenance
ProposalProvenance.description:
Who decided the proposal (PRD M5 D11/D13; M7 #31): a rule, exact
payee history, the AI classifier (unreachable until M9's Penny — v0
deterministically abstains), the transfer detector (M7 CP4 — it matched
a counterpart transaction), or nobody (the empty proposal). Contributing
rules for tags/rename ride in provenance_detail regardless.

condition.description:
A ConditionSpec payload; amount.currency may be omitted and is filled
from the user's primary currency before storage.

action_mark_transfer.description:
Propose an untracked transfer (M6 CP4); mutually exclusive with
action_category_id — one rule, one classification stance.

RuleStatus.title: RuleStatus
RuleStatus.description:
Only ACTIVE rules are law (evaluated by the pipeline). PROPOSED and
DISMISSED exist for CP4's promotion: a proposed rule awaits consent, a
dismissed one is a tombstone that prevents eternal re-proposal.

password.description:
Breach corpus checking (HIBP) is the real gate and lands in CP4;
a length floor is the NIST-baseline backstop, not the defense.

display_name.description: Defaults to the email's local part.

amount_minor.description:
Signed from the account's perspective, same convention as the parent;
bounded to the int4 column width so an out-of-range amount is a 400.

category_id.description: NULL = an uncategorized line (legal — same rule as transactions).

memo.description: Optional free-form label. Empty is rejected — omit or null instead.

token.description:
Breach corpus checking (HIBP) is the real gate and lands in CP4;
a length floor is the NIST-baseline backstop, not the defense.

amount_minor.description:
Signed from the account's perspective — negative is money out.
Bounded to the int4 column width so an out-of-range amount is a 400,
never a database error.

tags.description:
The complete tag set for the transaction; reconciled (implicit-create
new names, detach removed ones). Present-and-empty clears all tags, and
an explicit null does the same (both mean "no tags"). Each
name is bounded to the same 100 chars POST /tags enforces on this table,
and the set is capped so one PATCH can't mint an unbounded tag list.

display_name.description:
An override of the raw description; NULL shows description_raw (an
override, never a copy). Empty is rejected — clear with null, not "".

reviewed.description: True sets reviewed_at to now; False clears it (back to the inbox).

TransferKind.title: TransferKind
TransferKind.description: Derived, never stored: both sides present = linked, one = untracked.

sessionCookie.description: A browser session; unsafe methods also require the x-csrftoken header.
sessionCookie.name: pinch_session
